rationale: Two independent cohorts with the same metric family, same outcome, and the same high-versus-low contrast from the original HB paper.

Three rows exist, but the SHHS free-baseline-CVD row from 2025 overlaps the earlier SHHS cohort family; default primary pooling keeps the two 2019 cohort-level rows and holds the nested SHHS subset as overlap-sensitive narrative evidence.
Only one independent standardized HB row is available for cardiovascular mortality.

Both studies report log-scale HB associations, but their composite endpoints differ materially: one includes CHD/HF/stroke/CV mortality whereas the other includes all-cause mortality/AMI/stroke/revascularization.
Two independent cohorts report per-1-SD HB associations for all-cause mortality with compatible adjusted HRs.
